# Laravel Vurb

> Laravel Vurb is the PHP bridge that transforms any Laravel application into a production MCP Server — zero TypeScript. MVA architecture, Presenters as egress firewalls, PHP 8.2 Attributes, Eloquent Model Bridge, FSM State Gates, DLP Redaction, full Governance Stack. Powered by Vurb.ts under the hood.

## What is Laravel Vurb?

Laravel Vurb is a PHP package by Vinkius Labs that bridges the Laravel ecosystem to the Model Context Protocol (MCP) via the **MVA (Model-View-Agent)** pattern. Instead of rewriting business logic in TypeScript, you write PHP classes with typed parameters, decorate them with attributes, and the framework compiles everything into a Schema Manifest consumed by a lightweight Vurb.ts daemon. The daemon speaks MCP natively — Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, and every MCP-compatible AI client connect instantly.

**Philosophy:** *Zero TypeScript for the developer. 100% of Vurb.ts under the hood.*

## ⚠️ Namespace Reference — ALWAYS use these exact imports

**Package root namespace:** `Vinkius\Vurb\`

Every class in this package lives under `Vinkius\Vurb\`. Never abbreviate to `Vurb\` alone.

### Base Classes (extend these)

```
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbTool;            // Generic tool
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbQuery;           // Read-only tool (readOnly: true)
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbMutation;        // Destructive tool
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbAction;          // Neutral tool
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbRouter;          // Group tools with prefix + middleware
use Vinkius\Vurb\Presenters\VurbPresenter;  // Egress firewall (extends JsonResource)
use Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\VurbMiddleware;  // Tool middleware interface
```

### Attributes (decorate classes/params)

```
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Param;          // Parameter schema
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Tool;           // Class-level name/desc override
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Description;    // Description override
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Instructions;   // Anti-hallucination rules
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Tags;           // Capability filtering
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Presenter;      // Link tool → Presenter
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Query;          // Verb attribute (alt to subclass)
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Cached;         // State Sync caching
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Stale;          // Always refetch
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Invalidates;    // Mutation invalidation
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\FsmBind;        // FSM state restriction
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Hidden;         // Exclude param from schema
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\AgentLimit;     // Truncation guardrails
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Concurrency;    // Max parallel executions
```

### Support Classes

```
use Vinkius\Vurb\Models\Concerns\HasVurbSchema;   // Eloquent trait
use Vinkius\Vurb\Models\ModelRegistry;             // Model registration
use Vinkius\Vurb\Facades\Vurb;                     // Facade
use Vinkius\Vurb\Testing\FakeVurbTester;           // In-process testing
use Vinkius\Vurb\Governance\ContractCompiler;      // Lockfile governance
use Vinkius\Vurb\Skills\SkillsRegistry;            // SKILL.md registry
use Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\AuditTrail;             // Built-in: logging
use Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\RateLimitVurb;          // Built-in: rate limiting
use Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\RequirePermission;      // Built-in: authorization
```

### User Application Namespace Convention

Tool classes go in `app/Vurb/Tools/` → `namespace App\Vurb\Tools;`
Presenters go in `app/Vurb/Presenters/` → `namespace App\Vurb\Presenters;`
Subdomain tools go in `app/Vurb/Tools/Crm/` → `namespace App\Vurb\Tools\Crm;`
Custom middleware go in `app/Vurb/Middleware/` → `namespace App\Vurb\Middleware;`

## Core Architecture: MVA (Model-View-Agent)

```
Model (Eloquent + HasVurbSchema) → View (VurbPresenter) → Agent (LLM)
   validates                        perceives               acts
```

The Model is any Eloquent model with the `HasVurbSchema` trait — its casts, hidden fields, fillable profiles, and descriptions become the domain schema. The Presenter (`VurbPresenter`) extends Laravel's `JsonResource` and adds `systemRules()`, `uiBlocks()`, and `suggestActions()` — the AI perception layer. Tools are PHP classes that extend `VurbTool` and implement `handle()` with typed parameters that auto-compile to JSON Schema.

## Bridge Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐
│  AI Client   │  MCP  │  Vurb.ts     │  HTTP  │  Laravel App │
│  (Cursor,    │◄─────►│  Daemon      │◄──────►│  (Tools,     │
│   Claude)    │ stdio │  (npx tsx)   │  JSON  │   Presenters)│
└──────────────┘       └──────────────┘       └──────────────┘
```

The daemon reads a compiled Schema Manifest from PHP tool definitions and proxies every tool call back to Laravel over HTTP at `/_vurb/*` endpoints. All endpoints are protected by `X-Vurb-Token` with timing-safe comparison.

## Installation

```bash
composer require vinkius-labs/laravel-vurb
php artisan vurb:install
```

The installer publishes config, creates `app/Vurb/Tools/`, `app/Vurb/Middleware/`, `app/Vurb/Presenters/`, installs the Node.js daemon dependencies (via `npm install` in `bin/daemon/`), generates `VURB_INTERNAL_TOKEN` in `.env`, and runs migrations.

## Project Structure

```
app/Vurb/
├── Tools/                    ← Tool classes (VurbTool, VurbQuery, VurbMutation, VurbAction)
│   ├── Router.php            ← Optional top-level router
│   ├── GetCustomerProfile.php
│   ├── Crm/
│   │   ├── Router.php        ← Namespace router (prefix: 'crm', shared middleware)
│   │   ├── GetLead.php
│   │   └── UpdateLead.php
│   └── Billing/
│       ├── Router.php
│       └── GetInvoice.php
├── Presenters/               ← VurbPresenter classes (extends JsonResource)
│   ├── CustomerPresenter.php
│   └── InvoicePresenter.php
├── Middleware/                ← VurbMiddleware implementations
│   └── RequireCrmAccess.php
└── Skills/                   ← SKILL.md files for AI agent capabilities
    └── laravel-vurb-development/
        └── SKILL.md
```

## VurbTool — The Base Tool Class

Every tool extends `VurbTool` and implements `handle()` with typed parameters. Typed primitive parameters become the tool's JSON Schema input. Non-primitive type hints are resolved from Laravel's service container (dependency injection).

```php
namespace App\Vurb\Tools;

use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Param;
use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbTool;

class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbTool
{
    public function description(): string
    {
        return 'Retrieve a customer profile by ID.';
    }

    public function verb(): string
    {
        return 'query';
    }

    public function handle(
        #[Param(description: 'The customer ID', example: 42)]
        int $id,
    ): array {
        return \App\Models\Customer::findOrFail($id)->toArray();
    }
}
```

### VurbTool Methods

| Method | Return | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name(): string` | Tool name | Auto-inferred from class name. Override to set custom name. |
| `description(): string` | Description | **Required.** LLM-facing description of what the tool does. |
| `instructions(): ?string` | Instructions | Anti-hallucination instructions injected as `[INSTRUCTIONS]`. |
| `tags(): array` | Tags | Capability filtering labels. |
| `verb(): string` | Verb | `'query'` (readOnly), `'mutation'` (destructive), `'action'` (neutral). |
| `$middleware: array` | Middleware | Per-tool middleware class names. |
| `inferNameFromClass(): string` | Name | `GetCustomerProfile` → `customers.get_profile`. |

### Semantic Verb Subclasses

| Class | Verb | Annotations |
|---|---|---|
| `VurbQuery` | `query` | `readOnly: true`, `idempotent: true` |
| `VurbMutation` | `mutation` | `destructive: true` |
| `VurbAction` | `action` | No defaults (neutral) |

### Name Inference

Tool names are auto-inferred from the class name by extracting verb prefixes and pluralizing the subject:

| Class Name | Inferred Name |
|---|---|
| `GetCustomerProfile` | `customers.get_profile` |
| `CreateInvoice` | `invoices.create` |
| `ListOrders` | `orders.list` |
| `SearchProducts` | `products.search` |
| `ProcessPayment` | `payments.process` |
| `SendNotification` | `notifications.send` |

Recognized verb prefixes: `Get`, `List`, `Search`, `Create`, `Update`, `Delete`, `Remove`, `Activate`, `Deactivate`, `Process`, `Send`, `Cancel`, `Approve`, `Reject`.

Override: `public function name(): string { return 'my.custom_name'; }`

### Dependency Injection in handle()

Non-primitive type hints are resolved from Laravel's service container. Only primitive types (`int`, `string`, `float`, `bool`, `array`) become part of the JSON Schema input:

```php
public function handle(
    int $id,                                    // ← input schema parameter
    \App\Services\CrmGateway $crm,              // ← injected by container
    \Illuminate\Cache\Repository $cache,        // ← injected by container
): array {
    return $cache->remember("customer.{$id}", 60, fn () => $crm->find($id));
}
```

## PHP Attributes — Full Reference

### @Tool — Class-level override

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Tool;

#[Tool(name: 'custom.tool_name', description: 'Override description')]
class MyTool extends VurbTool { }
```

| Parameter | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | `?string` | Override auto-inferred name |
| `description` | `?string` | Override `description()` method |

### @Param — Parameter-level schema

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Param;

public function handle(
    #[Param(description: 'The customer ID', example: 42)]
    int $id,

    #[Param(description: 'Filter by status', items: ['active', 'inactive'])]
    ?string $status = null,
): array { }
```

| Parameter | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `description` | `?string` | Parameter description for JSON Schema |
| `example` | `mixed` | Example value for the LLM |
| `items` | `string\|array\|null` | Enum items for string parameters |

### @Description — Class/Method/Property description

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Description;

#[Description('Deep search across all customer records')]
class SearchCustomers extends VurbTool { }
```

### @Instructions — Anti-hallucination

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Instructions;

#[Instructions('Only call when the user explicitly asks for a customer lookup. Never infer IDs.')]
class SearchCustomers extends VurbTool { }
```

### @Tags — Capability filtering

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Tags;

#[Tags('crm', 'search', 'public')]
class SearchCustomers extends VurbTool { }
```

### @Presenter — Link tool to Presenter

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Presenter;

#[Presenter(CustomerPresenter::class)]
class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbTool { }
```

### @Query, @Mutation, @Action — Verb attributes (alternative to subclass)

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Query;

#[Query]
class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbTool { }
// Equivalent to extending VurbQuery
```

### @Cached — State Sync caching

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Cached;

#[Cached]                    // cache forever (immutable)
class GetCountries extends VurbQuery { }

#[Cached(ttl: 120)]        // stale after 120 seconds
class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbQuery { }
```

### @Stale — Always refetch

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Stale;

#[Stale]                     // never cache, always fresh
class GetBalance extends VurbQuery { }
```

### @Invalidates — Mutation invalidation patterns

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Invalidates;

#[Invalidates('customers.*', 'reports.*')]
class UpdateCustomer extends VurbMutation { }
```

### @FsmBind — FSM state restriction

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\FsmBind;

#[FsmBind(states: ['confirmed'], event: 'PAY')]
class ProcessPayment extends VurbMutation { }
// Tool only visible when FSM is in 'confirmed' state.
// On successful execution, triggers 'PAY' event → transitions FSM.
```

| Parameter | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `states` | `array` | FSM states where tool is visible |
| `event` | `?string` | Event to trigger on successful execution |

### @Hidden — Exclude parameter from schema

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Hidden;

public function handle(
    int $id,
    #[Hidden] string $internalFlag = 'default',
): array { }
// 'internalFlag' is not visible to the LLM
```

### @AgentLimit — Truncation guardrails

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\AgentLimit;

#[AgentLimit(max: 50, warningMessage: 'Use filters to narrow results.')]
class ListCustomers extends VurbQuery { }
```

### @Concurrency — Max parallel executions

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Concurrency;

#[Concurrency(max: 3)]
class ProcessPayment extends VurbMutation { }
```

## VurbPresenter — Egress Firewall & Perception Layer

The Presenter extends Laravel's `JsonResource` and controls what data reaches the AI agent. Every Presenter has four layers:

```php
namespace App\Vurb\Presenters;

use Vinkius\Vurb\Presenters\VurbPresenter;

class CustomerPresenter extends VurbPresenter
{
    /**
     * Egress Firewall — only these fields reach the AI.
     */
    public function toArray($request): array
    {
        return [
            'id'   => $this->id,
            'name' => $this->name,
            'plan' => $this->plan,
            // 'email' deliberately omitted — PII never reaches the LLM
        ];
    }

    /**
     * JIT System Rules — travel with data, not in the global prompt.
     */
    public function systemRules(): array
    {
        return [
            'Never reveal the customer email address.',
            'Always use the customer name in responses.',
            'For billing questions, suggest the billing.get_invoice tool.',
        ];
    }

    /**
     * Server-rendered UI blocks — deterministic visualizations.
     */
    public function uiBlocks(): array
    {
        return [
            ['type' => 'summary', 'title' => $this->name, 'subtitle' => "Plan: {$this->plan}"],
        ];
    }

    /**
     * Action affordances — HATEOAS for AI agents.
     */
    public function suggestActions(): array
    {
        return [
            ['tool' => 'customers.update', 'reason' => 'Edit customer details'],
            ['tool' => 'billing.get_invoice', 'reason' => 'View billing history'],
        ];
    }
}
```

### Presenter Layers

| Method | What It Does | Wire Format |
|---|---|---|
| `toArray($request)` | **Egress firewall** — only declared fields reach the AI | `data` in response |
| `systemRules()` | **JIT context injection** — rules the LLM must follow for THIS response | `systemRules` array |
| `uiBlocks()` | **Server-rendered UI** — charts, summaries, tables | `uiBlocks` array |
| `suggestActions()` | **HATEOAS for AI** — what the agent should do next | `suggestActions` array |

### Linking Presenter to Tool

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Attributes\Presenter;

#[Presenter(CustomerPresenter::class)]
class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbQuery
{
    public function handle(int $id): \App\Models\Customer
    {
        return \App\Models\Customer::findOrFail($id);
        // ↑ Return the model — Presenter filters and enriches automatically
    }
}
```

## VurbRouter — Group & Namespace Tools

Place a `Router.php` in a tools subdirectory. All tools in that directory inherit the router's prefix, middleware, and tags.

```php
namespace App\Vurb\Tools\Crm;

use Vinkius\Vurb\Tools\VurbRouter;

class Router extends VurbRouter
{
    public string $prefix = 'crm';
    public string $description = 'CRM operations — leads, contacts, deals';
    public array $middleware = [
        \App\Vurb\Middleware\RequireCrmAccess::class,
    ];
}
```

### Router Properties

| Property | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `$prefix` | `string` | Namespace prefix (e.g., `'crm'` → `crm.get_lead`) |
| `$description` | `string` | Description for the tool group |
| `$middleware` | `array` | Middleware applied to ALL tools in the directory |

## VurbMiddleware — Tool-Level Middleware

Implement the `VurbMiddleware` interface. Context array contains `tool`, `input`, `request`, `user`.

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\VurbMiddleware;

class RequireCrmAccess implements VurbMiddleware
{
    public function handle(array $context, \Closure $next): mixed
    {
        if (! $context['user']?->canAccessCrm()) {
            return ['error' => true, 'code' => 'FORBIDDEN', 'message' => 'CRM access required.'];
        }
        return $next($context);
    }
}
```

### Three Middleware Layers (merged automatically)

| Layer | Scope | Configuration |
|---|---|---|
| **Global** | All tools | `config('vurb.middleware')` array |
| **Router** | Tools in directory | `VurbRouter::$middleware` |
| **Per-tool** | Single tool | `VurbTool::$middleware` |

### Built-in Middleware

| Class | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\AuditTrail` | Logs tool name, input, user, latency, error status |
| `Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\RateLimitVurb` | 60 requests/minute per tool+user (uses Laravel RateLimiter) |
| `Vinkius\Vurb\Middleware\RequirePermission` | Laravel Gate authorization with variadic permissions |

## Eloquent Model Bridge — HasVurbSchema

Add the `HasVurbSchema` trait to any Eloquent model to include it in the Schema Manifest. The `ModelBridge` extracts fields, types, casts, hidden attributes, fillable profiles, and custom descriptions.

```php
namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
use Vinkius\Vurb\Models\Concerns\HasVurbSchema;

class Customer extends Model
{
    use HasVurbSchema;

    protected $hidden = ['password', 'remember_token'];

    protected $casts = [
        'plan' => PlanEnum::class,
        'email_verified_at' => 'datetime',
    ];

    public array $vurbDescriptions = [
        'name' => 'Full legal name of the customer',
        'plan' => 'Subscription tier: free, pro, or enterprise',
    ];
}
```

### ModelRegistry

Models are auto-discovered or manually registered:

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Models\ModelRegistry;

$registry = app(ModelRegistry::class);
$registry->register(\App\Models\Customer::class);
$registry->register(\App\Models\Invoice::class, 'Invoice');  // custom alias
$compiled = $registry->compileAll();  // all models compiled to manifest format
```

## FSM State Gate — Temporal Tool Governance

Configure a finite state machine in `config/vurb.php`. Tools bound with `#[FsmBind]` are physically removed from the manifest when the FSM is not in the specified states.

```php
// config/vurb.php
'fsm' => [
    'id'      => 'order_flow',
    'initial' => 'draft',
    'store'   => 'cache',   // 'cache' or 'database'
    'states'  => [
        'draft'      => ['on' => ['CONFIRM' => 'confirmed']],
        'confirmed'  => ['on' => ['PAY' => 'paid', 'CANCEL' => 'cancelled']],
        'paid'       => ['on' => ['SHIP' => 'shipped']],
        'shipped'    => ['on' => ['DELIVER' => 'delivered']],
        'delivered'  => [],
        'cancelled'  => [],
    ],
],
```

```php
#[FsmBind(states: ['confirmed'], event: 'PAY')]
class ProcessPayment extends VurbMutation
{
    // Only visible when FSM is in 'confirmed' state
    // On success, triggers PAY → transitions to 'paid'
}

#[FsmBind(states: ['paid'], event: 'SHIP')]
class ShipOrder extends VurbAction
{
    // Only visible after payment
}
```

The AI cannot call `ProcessPayment` until the order is confirmed. The tool does not exist in the manifest until the FSM reaches the right state. This prevents hallucination-driven out-of-order operations.

## DLP Redaction — PII Protection

Configure regex patterns for automatic PII stripping from every tool response:

```php
// config/vurb.php
'dlp' => [
    'enabled'  => true,
    'strategy' => 'mask',       // 'mask', 'remove', or 'hash'
    'patterns' => [
        '/\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b/'                           => 'SSN',
        '/\b\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}\b/'     => 'credit_card',
        '/\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}\b/i' => 'email',
    ],
],
```

Defense in depth: Presenter egress filtering + DLP pattern matching. Even if a tool developer forgets to filter in `toArray()`, the DLP engine catches it.

## State Sync — Cache Epistemic Policies

Control how the AI agent caches tool results:

```php
// config/vurb.php
'state_sync' => [
    'default'  => 'stale',                       // default: never cache
    'policies' => [
        'geo.*'       => ['policy' => 'cached'],  // reference data: forever
        'billing.*'   => ['policy' => 'stale'],    // always fresh
        'customers.*' => ['policy' => 'stale-after', 'ttl' => 120],
    ],
],
```

Inline per-tool via attributes: `#[Cached]`, `#[Cached(ttl: 120)]`, `#[Stale]`, `#[Invalidates('customers.*')]`.

## Governance & Introspection

### Contract Compiler

Generates per-tool contracts with SHA-256 digests:

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Governance\ContractCompiler;

$contracts = app(ContractCompiler::class)->compileContracts();
// Returns: ['customers.get_profile' => ['name' => ..., 'verb' => ..., 'digest' => 'sha256:...']]
```

### Lockfile Generator

CI gate for schema drift detection:

```bash
php artisan vurb:lock          # Generate vurb.lock
php artisan vurb:lock --check  # Exit 0 if unchanged, exit 1 if drift
```

### Dynamic Manifest — RBAC Filtering

```php
// config/vurb.php
'introspection' => [
    'enabled' => true,
    'filter'  => function (array $manifest, array $context) {
        if (($context['role'] ?? 'viewer') !== 'admin') {
            unset($manifest['tools']['billing']);
        }
        return $manifest;
    },
],
```

## Observability

Zero configuration. If Telescope or Pulse is installed, tool executions are recorded automatically.

| Integration | What's Recorded |
|---|---|
| **Telescope** (`VurbWatcher`) | Tool name, input, latency, presenter, rules, errors |
| **Pulse** (`VurbRecorder`) | Tool execution count, avg latency per tool |
| **Events** | `ToolExecuted`, `ToolFailed`, `DaemonStarted`, `DaemonStopped`, `ManifestCompiled`, `StateInvalidated` |

## Testing — FakeVurbTester & MvaTestResult

Runs the full tool pipeline in-process. No daemon, no HTTP.

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Testing\FakeVurbTester;

$result = FakeVurbTester::for(GetCustomerProfile::class)
    ->call(['id' => 42]);

$result->assertSuccessful();
$result->assertDataHasKey('name');
$result->assertDataMissingKey('email');         // egress firewall assertion
$result->assertDataEquals('name', 'Jane Doe');
$result->assertHasSystemRule('Never reveal the customer email address.');
$result->assertHasUiBlock('summary');
$result->assertSuggestsTool('customers.update');
```

### MvaTestResult Assertions

| Method | What It Validates |
|---|---|
| `assertSuccessful()` | No error occurred |
| `assertIsError(?string $code)` | Error with optional code (`VALIDATION_ERROR`, `RATE_LIMITED`, `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`) |
| `assertDataHasKey(string)` | Response contains key |
| `assertDataMissingKey(string)` | Egress firewall — sensitive key NOT in response |
| `assertDataEquals(string, mixed)` | Value equality check |
| `assertHasSystemRule(string)` | JIT rule is present in response |
| `assertHasSystemRules()` | Response has at least one system rule |
| `assertHasUiBlocks()` | Response has at least one UI block |
| `assertHasSuggestActions()` | Response has at least one suggested action |
| `assertSuggestsTool(string)` | Specific tool is suggested |
| `latency()` | Returns execution time in milliseconds |

### Testing with Middleware

```php
$result = FakeVurbTester::for(GetLead::class)
    ->withMiddleware([\App\Vurb\Middleware\RequireCrmAccess::class])
    ->call(['id' => 1]);

$result->assertIsError('FORBIDDEN');
```

## Artisan Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `vurb:install` | Setup: publish config, create dirs, npm install, generate token, run migrations |
| `vurb:serve` | Compile manifest, start Vurb.ts daemon, monitor process |
| `vurb:make-tool {Name}` | Generate tool scaffold (`--query`, `--mutation`, `--action`, `--router`) |
| `vurb:make-presenter {Name}` | Generate Presenter scaffold (`--collection`) |
| `vurb:manifest` | Compile and display Schema Manifest (`--json`, `--write`) |
| `vurb:inspect` | Inspect tools, schemas, demo payloads (`--tool=`, `--schema`, `--demo`) |
| `vurb:lock` | Generate `vurb.lock` for CI governance (`--check`) |
| `vurb:health` | Check daemon, Node.js, bridge, tools health |

## Bridge Protocol

The daemon communicates with Laravel over HTTP at `/_vurb/*`:

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/_vurb/execute/{toolName}/handle` | POST | Execute a tool |
| `/_vurb/schema/refresh` | POST | Recompile Schema Manifest |
| `/_vurb/state/transition` | POST | FSM state transition |
| `/_vurb/health` | GET | Health check |

All endpoints protected by `ValidateVurbToken` middleware (timing-safe `hash_equals`).

### Response Format

```json
{
  "data": { "id": 42, "name": "Jane Doe", "plan": "enterprise" },
  "meta": { "request_id": "a1b2c3", "latency_ms": 12.34, "tool": "customers.get_profile" },
  "systemRules": ["Never reveal the customer email address."],
  "uiBlocks": [{ "type": "summary", "title": "Jane Doe" }],
  "suggestActions": [{ "tool": "customers.update", "reason": "Edit details" }]
}
```

### Schema Manifest

```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "server": { "name": "my-app", "version": "1.0.0" },
  "bridge": { "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000", "prefix": "/_vurb" },
  "tools": {
    "customers": [
      {
        "name": "customers.get_profile",
        "description": "Retrieve a customer profile by ID.",
        "inputSchema": { "type": "object", "properties": { "id": { "type": "integer" } }, "required": ["id"] },
        "annotations": { "verb": "query", "readOnly": true },
        "middleware": []
      }
    ]
  },
  "presenters": {},
  "models": {},
  "stateSync": { "default": "stale", "policies": {} },
  "fsm": null,
  "skills": []
}
```

## Configuration Reference

```php
// config/vurb.php
return [
    'server' => [
        'name'        => env('VURB_SERVER_NAME', env('APP_NAME', 'laravel')),
        'version'     => env('VURB_SERVER_VERSION', '1.0.0'),
        'description' => env('VURB_SERVER_DESCRIPTION', 'MCP Server powered by Laravel Vurb'),
    ],

    'internal_token' => env('VURB_INTERNAL_TOKEN'),

    'tools' => [
        'path'      => app_path('Vurb/Tools'),
        'namespace' => 'App\\Vurb\\Tools',
    ],

    'exposition'  => 'flat',                              // 'flat' or 'grouped'
    'transport'   => env('VURB_TRANSPORT', 'stdio'),      // 'stdio', 'sse', 'streamable-http'

    'daemon' => [
        'manifest_path' => storage_path('app/vurb/manifest.json'),
        'node_path'     => env('VURB_NODE_PATH'),
        'npx_path'      => env('VURB_NPX_PATH'),
        'port'          => env('VURB_DAEMON_PORT'),
    ],

    'bridge' => [
        'base_url' => env('VURB_BRIDGE_URL', 'http://127.0.0.1:8000'),
        'prefix'   => '/_vurb',
        'timeout'  => 30,
    ],

    'middleware' => [],

    'state_sync' => [
        'default'  => 'stale',
        'policies' => [],
    ],

    'fsm' => null,

    'introspection' => [
        'enabled' => false,
        'filter'  => null,
    ],

    'dlp' => [
        'enabled'  => false,
        'patterns' => [],
        'strategy' => 'mask',
    ],

    'observability' => [
        'telescope' => true,
        'pulse'     => true,
        'events'    => true,
    ],
];
```

## Facade API

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Facades\Vurb;

Vurb::discover();           // All discovered tool instances
Vurb::compileManifest();    // Compiled manifest array
Vurb::isHealthy();          // Daemon health check (bool)
Vurb::discovery();          // ToolDiscovery singleton
Vurb::compiler();           // ManifestCompiler singleton
Vurb::daemon();             // DaemonManager singleton
Vurb::health();             // HealthCheck singleton
Vurb::presenters();         // PresenterRegistry singleton
Vurb::models();             // ModelRegistry singleton
```

## Skills Registry

Discover and register SKILL.md files from `app/Vurb/Skills/`:

```php
use Vinkius\Vurb\Skills\SkillsRegistry;

$skills = app(SkillsRegistry::class);
$skills->discover();                    // Scan app/Vurb/Skills/ for SKILL.md files
$skills->all();                         // All registered skills
$skills->get('laravel-vurb-development'); // Get specific skill
$skills->compileAll();                  // Compile for Schema Manifest
```

## Common Anti-Patterns — What NOT to Do

### ❌ Returning raw data without a Presenter

```php
// WRONG — no egress firewall, email leaks to LLM
public function handle(int $id): array {
    return Customer::findOrFail($id)->toArray();
}

// CORRECT — Presenter strips undeclared fields
#[Presenter(CustomerPresenter::class)]
class GetCustomerProfile extends VurbQuery {
    public function handle(int $id): Customer {
        return Customer::findOrFail($id);
    }
}
```

### ❌ Putting rules in the global prompt

```php
// WRONG — rules in config, not attached to data
// CORRECT — rules travel WITH the response via Presenter::systemRules()
```

### ❌ Using VurbTool when you know the verb

```php
// WRONG — generic tool, missing semantic annotations
class GetBalance extends VurbTool {
    public function verb(): string { return 'query'; }
}

// CORRECT — semantic subclass, readOnly auto-set
class GetBalance extends VurbQuery { }
```

### ❌ Skipping #[Param] for non-obvious parameters

```php
// WRONG — LLM guesses what 'q' means
public function handle(string $q, int $limit = 10): array { }

// CORRECT — explicit description + example
public function handle(
    #[Param(description: 'Search query — name, email, or phone', example: 'jane@acme.com')]
    string $query,
    #[Param(description: 'Maximum results to return')]
    int $limit = 10,
): array { }
```

## Quick Reference

| I want to... | Use |
|---|---|
| Create a read-only tool | Extend `VurbQuery` |
| Create a write tool | Extend `VurbMutation` |
| Create a neutral tool | Extend `VurbAction` |
| Shape what the AI sees | Create `VurbPresenter`, link with `#[Presenter]` |
| Group tools by domain | Create `VurbRouter` in subdirectory |
| Add auth middleware | Implement `VurbMiddleware`, add to router or tool `$middleware` |
| Expose an Eloquent model | Add `HasVurbSchema` trait |
| Cache tool results | `#[Cached]` or `#[Cached(ttl: 120)]` |
| Mark as volatile | `#[Stale]` |
| Invalidate on mutation | `#[Invalidates('customers.*')]` |
| Restrict to FSM state | `#[FsmBind(states: ['confirmed'], event: 'PAY')]` |
| Redact PII | Enable `dlp.enabled` in config, add patterns |
| Test full MVA pipeline | `FakeVurbTester::for(Tool::class)->call([...])` |
| Generate lockfile | `php artisan vurb:lock` |
| Check schema drift | `php artisan vurb:lock --check` |
| Start MCP server | `php artisan vurb:serve` |
| Scaffold a tool | `php artisan vurb:make-tool Name --query` |
| Scaffold a presenter | `php artisan vurb:make-presenter Name` |
